
### overflow and undefined behavior 


* C standard: some things are <i>undefined behavior</i>
* C compiler can do anything in those cases 
<hr class="vspace" />
* <i>signed integer overflow</i> is undefined
* <i>unsigned integer overflow</i> must wrap around

### undefined behavior surprise {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

```c
void found_overflow() __attribute__((noreturn));
void found_nonpositive_xy() __attribute__((noreturn));
int broken_check_add(int x, int y) {
    if (x > 0 && y > 0) {
        int result = x + y;
        if (result < x || result < y) {
            found_overflow();
        }
        return result;
    } else {
        found_nonpositive_xy();
    }
}
```

looks like it should detect overflow?

:::

::: {.column}

clang 22.1 -O assembly:
```gnuassembler
broken_check_add:
    testl   %edi, %edi
    setle   %cl  // cl <- x <= 0?
    testl   %esi, %esi
    setle   %dl  // dl <- y <= 0?
    orb     %cl, %dl 
    jne     .LBB0_2
        // goto .LBB0_2 if x <= 0 || y <= 0
    movl    %esi, %eax
    addl    %edi, %eax
        // return x + y
    retq
.LBB0_2:
    pushq   %rax
    xorl    %eax, %eax
    callq   found_nonpositive_xy@PLT
```

<em>missing `found_overflow()` call!</em>

:::

::::

### -fsanitize=undefined / -ftrapv (1) {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 150%; border: 3px dotted black;"}

```c
int x = INT_MAX - 1; int y = 5; printf("%d\n", x * y);
```

:::
 

* compile with <code>-fsanitize=undefined</code>: 

::: {style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex; margin-top: -1em;"}

<code>test.c:...: runtime error: signed integer overflow: 2147483646 * 5 cannot be represented in type 'int'</code>

:::

* compile with <code>-ftrapv</code>:

::: {style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex; margin-top: -1em;"}

<code>Aborted (core dumped)</code>

::::


### -fsanitize=unefined / -ftrapv (2) {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 150%; border: 3px dotted black;"}

```c
unsigned x = INT_MAX - 1; unsigned y = 5; printf("%u\n", x * y);
```

:::

* compile with <code>-fsanitize=undefined</code> or <code>-ftrapv</code>: <em>NO ERROR</em>
  * defined by C standard to wrap around



### in Rust (1) 

```rust
fn foo(x: usize) -> usize { x * 2 }
fn main() {
    let x = usize::MAX - 10;
    println!("{}", foo(x));
}
```
<hr>
in debug mode:

```{style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex;"}
thread 'main' panicked at src/main.rs:1:29:
attempt to multiply with overflow
```
<hr>
in release mode:
```{style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex;"}
18446744073709551594
```

### in Rust (2)


```rust
let x = usize::MAX - 10; let y = 10usize;
println!("{} {}", x.saturating_mul(2), y.saturating_mul(2));
```

```{style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex;"}
18446744073709551615 20
```
[18446744073709551615==usize::MAX]{style="font-size: 60%"}

<hr>

```rust
println!("{:?} {:?}", x.checked_mul(2), y.checked_mul(2));
```

```{style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex;"}
None Some(30)
```

<hr>

```rust
println!("{} {}", x.wrapping_mul(2), y.wrapping_mul(2));
```

```{style="border-left: darkred 5px solid; padding-left: 1ex;"}
18446744073709551594 20
```
[18446744073709551594==usize::MAX-21]{style="font-size: 60%"}
